options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: medium_ending_required
options.description: Whether watching the medium ending (defeat Maxim in Castle A) is required for goal completion.

options.name: worst_ending_required
options.description: Whether watching the worst ending (defeat Maxim in Castle B with JB's and MK's Bracelets unequipped) is required for goal completion.

options.name: best_ending_required
options.description:
Whether watching the best ending (defeat Maxim in Castle B with JB's and MK's Bracelets equipped) is required for goal completion.
Will be forced on if no other goal requirement is enabled.

options.name: furniture_amount_required
options.description: How many pieces of furniture are required to be found and set for goal completion. Furniture will be irrelevant if set to 0.

options.name: area_shuffle
options.description:
Randomizes where every transition to a different named area leads.
Separate: Both castles will have their areas kept separate from each other.
Combined: Both castles will have their areas mixed into one.

options.name: decouple_transitions
options.description: Whether transition entrances should be decoupled from exits if Area Shuffle is enabled. Going back through an area transition will send you somewhere completely different from whence you came.

options.name: link_door_types
options.description: Whether special door types should only be linked with each other (all skull doors link to other skull doors and all MK's Bracelet doors link to other MK's Bracelet doors).

options.name: early_lizard
options.description: Ensures you will find Lizard Tail in the multiworld's Sphere 1 somewhere, making the harder paths out less likely.

options.name: spellbound_boss_logic
options.description:
Makes certain bosses that are considered "medium" or "hard" in difficulty logically expect spell books to get past. See the Game Page for information on which bosses are considered what difficulty.
None: No boss expects any number of spell books.
Easy: Medium bosses expect 2 spell books and hard bosses expect 3 spell books.
Normal: Medium bosses expect 1 spell book and hard bosses expect 2 spell books.

options.name: castle_warp_condition
options.description:
The condition for allowing usage of all the warp room round gates to travel between castles.
None: No condition; the gates are all usable from the beginning.
Bracelet: The gates will unlock upon finding JB's Bracelet. There's no need to equip it.
Death: The gates will unlock after talking to Death at Clock Tower A like in the vanilla game.

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: double_sided_warps
options.description: Allows changing castles at a round warp gate without needing to fulfill the cross-castle warp condition if the warp rooms on both sides of it have been reached independently of each other.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
